task: We’ve got a storm system moving in, and I’m trying to decide what’s worth doing ahead of time so I’m not scrambling if the lights go out. Can you help me make a practical checklist for the house, food, and anything important to set aside first?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a practical, no-fluff storm prep checklist you can use.

## Do first
- [ ] Charge phones, power banks, laptops, and any battery packs
- [ ] Fill vehicle gas tank
- [ ] Set aside flashlights/headlamps and check batteries
- [ ] Locate and test backup power gear:
  - [ ] Generator
  - [ ] Extension cords
  - [ ] Fuel can
  - [ ] Battery packs / inverter
- [ ] Put important documents in one easy-to-grab folder:
  - [ ] IDs
  - [ ] Insurance papers
  - [ ] Medical info
  - [ ] Cash
- [ ] Set aside medications:
  - [ ] Daily meds
  - [ ] Inhalers
  - [ ] EpiPens
  - [ ] Any refrigerated meds
- [ ] Bring in or secure anything outside that can blow around
- [ ] Close and latch windows, doors, garage, and storm shutters if you have them
- [ ] Check sump pump / drains / gutters if flooding is possible

## House
- [ ] Move valuables up off the floor if water is a risk
- [ ] Put towels, buckets, and trash bags where they’re easy to reach
- [ ] Know where the main water shutoff and breaker panel are
- [ ] Unplug sensitive electronics if lightning/outages are likely
- [ ] Freeze containers of water if you want extra ice for the fridge/freezer

## Food and water
- [ ] Fill clean containers with drinking water
- [ ] Buy or set aside shelf-stable food that needs no cooking:
  - [ ] Canned soup/beans/tuna
  - [ ] Crackers
  - [ ] Peanut butter
  - [ ] Granola bars
  - [ ] Ready-to-eat meals
- [ ] Make sure you have food that can be eaten cold if needed
- [ ] Use or freeze perishables you won’t want to lose
- [ ] Check freezer space so items are packed tightly; a fuller freezer holds cold longer

## Fridge/freezer timing
- [ ] If power goes out, keep fridge/freezer closed as much as possible
- [ ] Fridge: usually safe about 4 hours unopened
- [ ] Freezer:
  - [ ] Full freezer: about 48 hours unopened
  - [ ] Half-full freezer: about 24 hours unopened
- [ ] If outage is likely to be long, move priority items to the coldest part of the freezer or into a cooler with ice

## Set aside first
If you only do a few things, make it these:
- [ ] Phones and backup batteries charged
- [ ] Flashlights/headlamps ready
- [ ] Meds packed
- [ ] Documents together
- [ ] Fuel can filled
- [ ] Water and easy food ready
- [ ] Generator and cords checked

If you want, I can turn this into a tighter printable checklist with **Today / If the storm track worsens / If power goes out** sections.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good, but I’d want it a little tighter and more specific. You’ve got the right idea, but I don’t need a bunch of generic storm-prep fluff.

What I’d want added:
- freezer/fridge plan for a real outage, not just the usual time estimates
- what to do with prescription meds that need refrigeration
- what paperwork actually matters if there’s damage later
- one short section for outside stuff, since that’s what gets blown around first
- a note about not trusting a generator you haven’t tested in a while

And I’d rather have it in a simple order I can use fast:
1. power and lights
2. food and water
3. papers/meds
4. outside and house
5. if the outage lasts more than a day

If you want, make it printable and plain. No extra commentary.

turns.agent_response:
## Storm Prep Checklist

### 1) Power and lights
- [ ] Charge phones, tablets, laptops, and power banks now
- [ ] Charge backup batteries and rechargeable lanterns
- [ ] Put flashlights/headlamps where you can reach them fast
- [ ] Check batteries in flashlights/headlamps
- [ ] Test generator now, not after the outage
- [ ] Make sure you have fuel for the generator
- [ ] Put extension cords with the generator gear
- [ ] Fill vehicle gas tank

### 2) Food and water
- [ ] Fill clean containers with drinking water
- [ ] Set aside shelf-stable food:
  - [ ] Canned soup/beans/tuna
  - [ ] Peanut butter
  - [ ] Crackers
  - [ ] Granola bars
  - [ ] Ready-to-eat meals
- [ ] Freeze a few containers of water for extra ice
- [ ] Use up or freeze perishables you do not want to lose
- [ ] Pack the freezer tightly if you can

### 3) Papers and meds
- [ ] Put these in one grab-and-go folder:
  - [ ] IDs
  - [ ] Insurance cards/policies
  - [ ] Medical info
  - [ ] Home/rent papers
  - [ ] Photos or a quick inventory of valuables
  - [ ] Cash
- [ ] Set aside all prescription meds
- [ ] Put refrigerated meds in a cooler with ice packs if the outage may last
- [ ] Keep a list of medication names, doses, and pharmacy numbers with the folder

### 4) Outside and house
- [ ] Bring in patio furniture, trash cans, tools, and loose items
- [ ] Secure anything that can blow around
- [ ] Close and latch windows and doors
- [ ] Move anything valuable off the floor if flooding is possible
- [ ] Know where the breaker panel and water shutoff are

### 5) If the outage lasts more than a day
- [ ] Keep fridge and freezer closed as much as possible
- [ ] Fridge: about 4 hours unopened
- [ ] Freezer: about 48 hours if full, about 24 hours if half-full
- [ ] If the fridge warms up, use the most perishable food first
- [ ] Move key food to a cooler with ice if needed
- [ ] Do not use a generator you have not tested recently

### First things to grab
- [ ] Phones and chargers
- [ ] Flashlights/headlamps
- [ ] Generator fuel and cords
- [ ] Meds
- [ ] Documents
- [ ] Water
- [ ] Easy food

user_simulator.text:
Better. Close enough to use, but I’d tighten two things and make one point a little firmer.

The med line needs to say what to do if the refrigerator meds can’t stay cold long enough — some of those can’t just sit out. And for the paperwork, I’d rather see only the stuff that matters after a claim: insurance policy, photos, receipts, serial numbers, and any doctor/pharmacy info for meds. Leave off the rest.

Also, I’d say the generator note should be more blunt: don’t count on one you haven’t run under load lately. A gas can full of stale fuel and a generator that won’t start is just clutter.

If you want, I can turn it into a one-page plain checklist with no extra wording at all.

turns.agent_response:
## Storm Prep Checklist

### 1) Power and lights
- [ ] Charge phones, tablets, laptops, and power banks now
- [ ] Charge backup batteries and rechargeable lanterns
- [ ] Put flashlights/headlamps where you can reach them fast
- [ ] Check batteries in flashlights/headlamps
- [ ] Fill vehicle gas tank
- [ ] Test generator **under load** now, not after the outage
- [ ] Make sure generator fuel is fresh
- [ ] Put extension cords with the generator gear

### 2) Food and water
- [ ] Fill clean containers with drinking water
- [ ] Set aside shelf-stable food:
  - [ ] Canned soup/beans/tuna
  - [ ] Peanut butter
  - [ ] Crackers
  - [ ] Granola bars
  - [ ] Ready-to-eat meals
- [ ] Freeze a few containers of water for extra ice
- [ ] Use up or freeze perishables you do not want to lose
- [ ] Pack the freezer tightly if you can

### 3) Papers and meds
- [ ] Put these in one grab-and-go folder:
  - [ ] Insurance policy
  - [ ] Photos of the house and belongings
  - [ ] Receipts for big-ticket items
  - [ ] Serial numbers for major items
  - [ ] Doctor/pharmacy info for medications
- [ ] Set aside all prescription meds
- [ ] Put refrigerated meds in a cooler with ice packs if the outage may last
- [ ] If a refrigerated med cannot stay cold long enough, call the pharmacy or doctor now for instructions

### 4) Outside and house
- [ ] Bring in patio furniture, trash cans, tools, and loose items
- [ ] Secure anything that can blow around
- [ ] Close and latch windows and doors
- [ ] Move anything valuable off the floor if flooding is possible
- [ ] Know where the breaker panel and water shutoff are

### 5) If the outage lasts more than a day
- [ ] Keep fridge and freezer closed as much as possible
- [ ] Fridge: about 4 hours unopened
- [ ] Freezer: about 48 hours if full, about 24 hours if half-full
- [ ] If the fridge warms up, use the most perishable food first
- [ ] Move key food to a cooler with ice if needed
- [ ] Do not count on a generator you have not run under load lately

### First things to grab
- [ ] Phones and chargers
- [ ] Flashlights/headlamps
- [ ] Generator fuel and cords
- [ ] Meds
- [ ] Documents
- [ ] Water
- [ ] Easy food

user_simulator.text:
That’s usable. Plain enough, and the order makes sense.

I’d keep it like that.
